trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Svizzera exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and chemicals. Sudafrica exports precious metals (gold, platinum, diamonds), vehicles, and mineral products. Swiss commodity trading firms handle significant Sudafrican mineral exports.

diplomatic.summary: Stable bilateral relations. Svizzera has supported post-apartheid Sudafrica's development. Cooperation in multilateral forums including WTO and UN. Sudafrica's growing BRICS alignment creates some political divergence with Svizzera's Western-aligned neutrality. Some tension over Sudafrica's ICJ case against Israele.

military.summary: No military alliance or significant defense cooperation. Svizzera's neutrality precludes formal military ties. Some Swiss firms supply equipment to Sudafrican security forces.

financial.summary: Swiss banks manage significant Sudafrican private wealth. Swiss commodity trading companies (Glencore) are major players in Sudafrican mining sector. Some bilateral FDI in mining, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Sudafrica exports some coal through global markets. Svizzera has provided technical assistance for Sudafrica's renewable energy transition and nuclear power program.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Swiss universities collaborate with Sudafrican institutions in infectious disease research, biodiversity, and materials science. Swiss government supports scientific capacity-building in Sudafrica.
